title: salix alba subsp. vitellina
intro: Salix alba subsp. vitellina, also known as white willow, is a subspecies of the white willow tree, native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and analgesic compounds such as salicin. The health benefits of Salix alba subsp. vitellina include relief from pain and inflammation, as well as reduced fever and improved cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or infusions, which are often used to treat various ailments, including arthritis and headaches.
